title: Spinach & Ricotta Stuffed Shells
description: These delicious and family-friendly stuffed shells are packed with creamy ricotta and healthy spinach, all baked in a savory tomato sauce. A perfect comforting meal that's easy to prepare and kid-approved!
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Cook pasta shells according to package directions until al dente. Drain and rinse with cold water to prevent sticking.
While pasta cooks, thaw and drain the frozen spinach very well, squeezing out as much excess water as possible.
In a large bowl, combine ricotta cheese, drained spinach, 1/4 cup of the grated Parmesan cheese, egg, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Mix until well combined.
Spread about 1 cup of marinara sauce in the bottom of a 9x13 inch baking dish.
Carefully stuff each cooked pasta shell with the ricotta-spinach mixture. Arrange the stuffed shells in a single layer in the baking dish.
Pour the remaining marinara sauce over the stuffed shells. Sprinkle with the shredded mozzarella cheese and the remaining 1/4 cup of Parmesan cheese.
Cover the baking dish with foil and bake for 20 minutes. Remove foil and bake for another 10-15 minutes, or until cheese is bubbly and lightly golden.
Let stand for 5 minutes before serving.
tips: To make it even easier, you can use a piping bag or a Ziploc bag with the corner snipped off to fill the shells quickly and neatly.
For a little extra flavor, sauté some diced onion or bell pepper and add it to the ricotta mixture.
If you have picky eaters, you can finely chop the spinach or even blend it into the ricotta mixture to hide it a bit more!
